<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鶴崎神社]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鶴崎神社]]
{{Expand Japanese|鶴崎神社|date=November 2025}}
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鶴崎神社]]


{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
|name = Tsurusaki Shrine
|image = {{image requested|cat=都窪郡}}
|location = 2220 Hayashima, Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan
|coordinates = {{coord|34|37|N|133|52|E|region:JP-33|display=inline,title}}
|deity = Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto (荒魂)
|status = 
|establishment = 1350 (Jōwa 6)
|architecture = 
|annual_festival = 
|rituals = 
}}

'''Tsurusaki Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|鶴崎神社}}, ''Tsurusaki Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in Hayashima Town, Tsukubo District, Okayama Prefecture, Japan. The shrine enshrines the deity Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto in his fierce spirit (荒魂).

== History ==
Tsurusaki Shrine was founded in 1350 (Jōwa 6) when the fierce spirit of Ōkibitsuhiko no Mikoto was transferred from [[Kibitsu Shrine]].

== Cultural Properties ==
The shrine houses several town-designated important cultural properties:
* '''Painting from Kan’ei-ji Temple, Ueno''' (1716)
* '''Framed Plaque of Hayashima Ten Views''' (1890)
* '''Stone Lanterns on the East Approach''' (1856)

== Nearby Facilities ==
* '''Hanagoza Hand-Weaving Museum'''

== References ==
* ''Shrines and Temples of the Land of Kibi'' by Sanyo Shimbun (2011)
* [http://turusaki2.web.fc2.com/ Tsurusaki Shrine Official Website]
== See Also ==
* [[Kibitsu Shrine]]
* [[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]]

{{Shinto Horizontal}}


{{translated page|ja|鶴崎神社 (早島町)|version=92434961|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:鶴崎神社]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[ja:鶴崎神社 (早島町)]]
{{wikidata link|Q11675995}}

[[Category:Shinto shrines in Okayama Prefecture]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
